title: Palicourea celiae
taxonomy: Palicourea celiae is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises approximately 6000 to 7000 species, many of which are economically and culturally significant. Palicourea celiae is further classified within the genus Palicourea, which includes approximately 300 species of flowering plants. The genus Palicourea is primarily distributed throughout the tropical regions of Central and South America. The exact classification of Palicourea celiae may vary slightly depending on the specific taxonomic system used.
uses: Palicourea celiae is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In some traditional remedies, it is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The leaves and roots of the plant are often used to create medicinal infusions. Further studies are needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Palicourea celiae for medicinal purposes.

intro: Palicourea celiae, also known as Celia, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea celiae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit potent biological activities. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in the production of herbal extracts, tinctures, and essential oils.
